title: Aleco Stusea

{{Quote|After all, Duron, Shaela and Guun Han are our greatest hunters… ''together,'' they can resist the dark side.|Aleco Stusea|<ref name="shadows" />}}
'''Aleco Stusea''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Near-Human]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served on the [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Stusea was one of several Masters who led the [[Jedi Enclave]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], joining the likes of [[Vrook Lamar]], [[Bala Nisi]], and [[Qual]]. After the official end of the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]], the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order's]] endeavor to eradicate [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-created [[Species/Legends|beasts]] [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-wide in the aftermath of the Great Sith War, in [[3993 BBY]], Stusea informed [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Duron Qel-Droma]], [[Shaela Nuur]], and [[Guun Han Saresh]] that they were being sent to wipe out [[Terentatek/Legends|terentateks]] on the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Although some of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Enclave Council disagreed with the [[Cleansing of Korriban|mission]], Stusea trusted in the abilities of the three Jedi and in the wisdom of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], which had chosen them for the task. However, Qel-Droma, Nuur, and Saresh would never return. Stusea served on the Enclave Council until the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Jedi Civil War]], in which she was presumed [[Death/Legends|killed]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Officially, yes, Duron, the Hunt is over. However, there is still ''one planet'' left to cleanse…|Aleco Stusea, to Duron Qel-Droma|<ref name="shadows" />}}
A [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Near-Human]], Aleco Stusea was, by [[3993 BBY]], a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and a member of the [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council|Jedi Council]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine's]] [[Jedi Enclave]]. In the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] launched the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]], aimed to eradicate the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-created monstrosities that still inhabited a number of worlds across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. In 3993 BBY, three years after the end of the war, the Great Hunt was declared officially over. However, shortly afterward, Stusea and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Enclave Council were told by [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] that there was still one remaining mission to complete. According to the High Council's wishes, Stusea and the Enclave Council called three [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]—[[Human/Legends|Humans]] [[Duron Qel-Droma]] and [[Shaela Nuur]], and [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Guun Han Saresh]], together believed to be the very best [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]] in the entire Order—and informed them that they had been chosen to [[Cleansing of Korriban|cleanse]] the Sith [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], a planet deeply steeped in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]], of the vicious and highly dangerous [[Terentatek/Legends|terentatek]] [[Species/Legends|beast]]. Stusea was the one who directly broke the news to the three Jedi, and the Knights were sent away on their mission.<ref name="shadows" />

[[File:Jediassembly.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Aleco Stusea sits on the Jedi Enclave Council.]]
After Qel-Droma, Nuur, and Saresh left, Stusea discussed the matter in the council chambers with the other Masters of the Enclave Council, including Humans [[Vrook Lamar]] and [[Bala Nisi]], and the [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] [[Qual]]. Despite the decree of the Jedi High Council, Lamar and Nisi disagreed with the Council ordering only the three Jedi to Korriban. Although acknowledging that sending the Jedi to Korriban was risky due to the strong dark side temptation of the world, Stusea expressed faith in the Jedi High Council's wisdom, believing that together the three hunters could resist the pull of the dark side. However, despite their reputations, Qel-Droma, Nuur, and Saresh would all [[Death/Legends|perish]] battling terentateks—Qel-Droma and Nuur on Korriban, and Saresh on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], after a dispute that ended in the Twi'lek leaving the group.<ref name="shadows" /> Stusea went on to serve on the Jedi Enclave Council for many more years, finally leaving the august body after the outbreak of the [[Jedi Civil War]] in [[3959 BBY]]. It was believed that Stusea was killed at some point in the war, which ended with the [[Battle of Rakata Prime]] in [[3956 BBY]].<ref name="cswe" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|However, as Qual said, we must abide by the Council's decisions—and have ''faith'' in their wisdom.|Aleco Stusea|<ref name="shadows" />}}
Holding a position of leadership on the Jedi Enclave Council, Aleco Stusea was welcoming and understanding of viewpoints counter to her own, and was quick to acknowledge when someone made a valid point. Stusea had faith in the wisdom of the Jedi High Council, although she admittedly did not always agree with its decisions. She also believed in Qel-Droma, Nuur, and Saresh, thinking that they could resist the Korriban's dark temptation by relying on one another. Stusea had [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and light skin.<ref name="shadows" />

==Equipment==
While serving on the Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council, Stusea wore a white [[Clothing/Legends|dress]] along with a golden [[Necklace/Legends|necklace]].<ref name="shadows" />

==Behind the scenes==
Aleco Stusea first appeared in "[[Shadows and Light]]," a story published in [[Star Wars Tales 23|''Star Wars Tales'' 23]], that was written by [[Joshua Ortega]], illustrated by [[Dustin Weaver]],<ref name="shadows" /> and released on [[May 11]], [[2005]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54696/star_wars_tales_1999_23|text=Star Wars Tales (1999) #23}}</ref> Although she was only named as "Aleco" in "Shadows and Light," Ortega gave her full name in a post on the [[Dark Horse Comics]] message boards.<ref name="darkhorse">{{DHBoards|url=viewtopic.php?p=105403|thread=Tales KOTOR story Jedi?|user=[[Joshua Ortega]]|date=2005-06-11|time=17:53|quote=Completely new Jedi in the story are Master Aleco Stusea (head of the Council on Dantooine), Bala Nisi (human female), Qual (a Selkath), and Cale Berkona (the Jedi in the terentatek's grip at the start).}}</ref> In [[2013]], this name was confirmed as [[canon]] within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity by [[Leland Chee]], who is in charge of the official [[Holocron continuity database]].<ref name="fb">{{Facebook|noprofile=1|author=[[Leland Chee|Chee, Leland]] ("Keeper of the Holocron")|date=2013-02-09|quote=Stusea is what I have in the Holocron based on our comic script, and if the author posted it on the Dark Horse boards, I'd say that's official enough.|url=permalink.php?story_fbid=508574639193685&id=201820953172172&comment_id=5343840&notif_t=share_comment|nobackup=1}}</ref>
